title: Ethical Implications of AI in Higher Education
content: The presentation discusses the ethical aspects of artificial intelligence within university-level education. It is presented by Li Wei, a Year 3 international student from a Digital Sociology class.

title: Contextual Landscape of AI
content: There is a significant shift in modern lecture halls due to Large Language Models (LLMs). This integration is widespread, with academic environments adopting AI methods. AI tools are now a standard scholarship practice.
key_claims: Modern lecture halls are undergoing a paradigm shift due to LLMs.
AI integration is widespread in academic environments.
Use of AI tools is now a standardized practice.

title: Escalating Student Adoption Rates
content: The slide presents data on the increasing use of AI among students, particularly in assessments. There is high global student AI usage, but minimal faculty integration.

title: Core Ethical Dilemmas
content: The slide highlights three main ethical issues: academic integrity, algorithmic bias, and cognitive decline. Issues include reliance on generative outputs, societal inequalities in AI datasets, and declining critical reasoning.
key_claims: Academic integrity is threatened by over-reliance on generative AI.
Cognitive decline is a risk due to delegated tasks to machines.

title: Synthesis & Strategic Outlook
content: The slide proposes strategies like transparency frameworks, assessment redesign, and addressing preparedness gaps. These aim to address ethical challenges and bridge student and faculty divides.
key_claims: Transparency frameworks for academic stakeholders are essential.
Assessment redesign focuses on oral defense and evaluations.
Bridging gaps between student adoption and faculty training is critical.
